# Flooding in Australia 

 In 2010, Queensland experienced its wettest December on record, leading to a prolonged period of extensive flooding.

slides.text:
## Global Mean Sea Level 

 The global sea level observed by satellites shows an upward trend of 3.3 mm per year, but a decline in 2011 due to the large amount of excess water on land.

slides.text:
## New South Wales, Australia 

 Copernicus Sentinel-1 radar images from March 7 and 19, 2021, show extensive flooded areas in blue. Areas burned by wildfires in the previous year are shown in brown.

### Caption 

 Contains modified Copernicus Sentinel data (2021), processed by ESA / NASA MODIS

slides.text: After months of high temperatures and low rainfall, the water level of Italy's Po River reached a record low in 2022. The drought in northern Italy impacted the region's agricultural production, which often relies on irrigation.

slides.text:
## A Passage Opens 

For centuries, the Northwest Passage between mainland Canada and its Arctic islands has held promise as a shorter sea route between Europe and Asia. But for most of this time it has proved an impenetrable barrier, locked fast in the grip of a frozen sea.

The pack ice defeated the Royal Navy in 1845 when Sir John Franklin’s expedition was lost. Eighteen search parties over the next thirty years failed to find any trace of him and his 130 crewmen. It wasn’t until 1906 that Roald Amundsen became the first to complete a route through the Northwest Passage, after a journey lasting three years.

Young Arctic sea ice viewed from an aircraft. Sea ice thinner than 50 cm is particularly important for weather and climate as it controls the exchange of heat and water between the ocean and atmosphere. (S Hendriks/AWI)
slides.altText: Arctic sea ice viewed from an aircraft

slides.text: Pressure ridge in thick Arctic sea ice, formed as two ice floes converge. (Seymour Laxon/CPOM/UCL)
slides.altText: Pressure ridge in Arctic sea ice

slides.text: Sea ice in Resolute Bay, Canada, from Sentinel-2. (Modified Copernicus Sentinel data (2019), processed by Pierre Markuse)
